item.title: Refresh - a comic about burnout
item.description: When I started working from home, I was comfy and happy in my jammies, I got to cook my lunch in my own kitchen, and I got to spend all day with my cat. However, I quickly realized I don't hate people as much as I thought I did and started to spin into a depressive state. I changed jobs, traveled a tiny bit, and things got a lot better. Thus, comic inspiration was born! :) Thanks for reading
item.dc:creator: sleepyheadkl

item.title: A country founded on racism, sexism, and genocide.
item.dc:creator: neoswhiterabbitt

item.title: Living my best life as a medieval peasant
item.description:
I recently visited Evermore Park in Pleasant Grove, Utah. This is a permanent park that is a perfect blend of Renaissance Faire and Live Action Role Play
Guests are encouraged to come in character and in costume! My name was Juniper. I just needed something to wear…
I’ve always wanted to learn to sew and make costumes and used the pandemic to really dive in. Both corsets and skirts were handmade by me!
The green was my first attempt and I’m so proud of it! Not perfect and my skills were definitely more refined by the navy one.
Evermore park also had a statue in their church graveyard of Saint Apollonia. The patron saint of teeth, which was so perfect for me because I’m a dental hygienist! See her forceps? Guests leave offerings to her for fortune and favor for good teeth.
This park is beautiful. So much love is put into it by the actors. It’s also ridiculously affordable, I would have easily paid triple the price for admission. You interact with characters to go on quests to earn gold and other treasures. One wizard told our fortunes from Pokémon Cards!
These are some of my treasures I won in the park. A dragon’s tear from the dragon trainers guild, a special gold doubloon from the pirates, a ruby, a key to the inn so we would always have a place to stay if we came back to Evermore, a ring from the finger of a fairy, and I kept one of the pieces of gold which is just painted rocks. I gave the rest of my gold back to the Evermore actors to recycle to new guests.
This is the Pigmyweed’s Inn, if you give the dwarf innkeeper Fergus 3 pieces of gold he will sing you a song. Since I visited the dragon trainers guild (which I joined) got a huge lifelike dragon! And they have baby dragons. There’s also jousts, archery, axe throwing, and more!
Stop in their attached restaurant and bar called Vander’s Keep. There I had some of the best venison of my life and amazing cocktails.
We were there on Fairy weekend when they had a lantern festival. You can choose to join the guild of fairies, pirates, knights, dragon trainers, and more!
TLDR - Go LARP in Utah FRONT PAGE EDIT: my goodness thank you guys so much! I’ve gotten lots of questions on what patterns I used and how to start sewing. I used Butterick 4669 as a base for the corset bodice. I had to modify it to fit my smaller bust, added the boning channels to give it structure just by eyeballing it, and enclosed the edges with bias tape - that part was all hand sewn because ✨I hate myself✨For the bones themselves I used a classic costumer’s trick of industrial strength zip ties. I taught myself how to sew through lots of YouTube. Rachel Maksy, Morgan Donner, and Bernadette Banner are my favorite sewing/costuming youtubers. No the corset is not uncomfortable and does not make it hard to breathe. It’s well fitted and tailored for me so it fits close but is not stifling. Big poofy sleeves and full skirts give the optical illusion of a small waist. It’s all about the silhouette. Happy sewing and costuming! And for everyone in the comments coming after me for not being “historically accurate” clearly I’m not trying to be. I was riddling with wizards and collecting moss from fairies for gods sake. “Medieval” encompasses a time frame of literally a thousand years (5th to 15th centuries CE) There is no such thing as 100% historical accuracy because we have such little documentation of historical and extant garments. What we do know is that people of all classes and time periods loved colors and patterns. Clearly this is fantasy and fun. Let people enjoy things

item.title: If you are travelling this summer…
item.description:
My friend is a flight attendant. She shared this post on her feed and I wanted to share it with you all.
My husband and I are travelling this summer from Canada to Europe with our niece. Starting to have second thoughts.
If you have any post-COVID travel stories, I’d like to hear them!
Travel safely!
item.dc:creator: ninjasenseivalkyrieprincess

item.title: Made a telemarketer hang up on me
item.description: He asked whether I would “please” vote for a GOP candidate this November. I responded “not until there is a candidate that will support a woman’s right to her own reproductive health choices.” This started a 10 minute back and forth with me angrily poking holes in his piss poor forced birth argument, with accompanying sources, and contradicting bible verses which he would then say “that depends on interpretation.” And reminded him several times that politicians serve all religions within their constituency, not just Christians. This is a human rights issue, not a religious one. I shared my own abortion story, married, on birth control, ectopic pregnancy that would have killed me without an abortion. He conceded there should be exceptions if the mother’s life is in jeopardy. So I asked who gets to make that decision? Who decides at exactly what point when the mother is in jeopardy? “Well I’m not a medical expert” EXACTLY I screamed. That’s when I really laid in on him, my son giggling down the hall listening to my rant. The call ended with him saying “well then you shouldn’t have gotten pregnant” and hanging up on me. So I guess fuck me for having marital relations with my husband. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯ To be clear, I support a woman’s right to chose regardless of her reasons. I?DO?NOT ?FUCKING ?CARE?WHY I got him to concede on one exception so I laid in on him with other “exceptions” that he has absolutely no right to make a decision on. I am beyond angry and I am absolutely fucking terrified. I’m angry at my fellow females that support forced birth. I’m angry we aren’t striking nationwide. I’m angry Republicunts have such a wider social media presence and news platforms. I’m angry that all I can do is nothing. Waiting for elections. Waiting for judges to make decisions. Waiting for other women to get as angry as I am. We will do nothing until all of our rights are revoked. I go to sleep angry. I wake up angry. It is on my mind 24/7. OUR BODIES, OUR CHOICE! FP Edit: Thanks for the support guys. For those commenting on my husband, he died in 2017. He became a Trumper shortly before his death, but prior to that, he’d have been super supportive and one of the first to riot and had participated in some before. I miss the man he was. He had a unique sense of humor so I was never sure if he actually believed and supported Trump or if he just enjoyed the ridiculousness of it and was joking just to rile people up.
item.dc:creator: saltysquash

item.title: Christians love to take things by force
item.description: Just a reminder of how Christianity became so popular today. During the reign of Charlemagne women were impaled on sharpened poles put in the vagina. Slowly over days the pole would travel the length of the body through the organs causing tremendous pain, simply because a woman was found collecting herbs in the forest. She was labeled a witch. Their screams could be heard for days as an example to those who would not accept the foreign faith. Christianity became so popular because of sheer terror of what would happen if it wasn't accepted. And people think the Nazis were horrible. This is still happening today in the form of collective social pressure. Say your a pagan and your immediately labeled dangerous, evil, and a threat to the world.
item.dc:creator: Lexicat

item.title: Just rewards
item.description: ? "When Clarence Thomas found the perfect bachelorette, and the couple announced their engagement, they raised eyebrows and blood pressure. This was, however, primarily among those who hadn't met the love-struck pair. "I can guarantee you I was surprised when I found out she was going with a black man," Ginni Thomas's uncle Ralph Knop said from his farm in Iowa. "It was unusual for us." "But he was so nice, we forgot he was black," her aunt Opal added, "and he treated her so well, all of his other qualities made up for his being black." ... ... ... At the wedding, which was held in a largely white Methodist church in Omaha, "there was some buzz in the congregation because people didn't realize he was black and there was a sort of a 'Huh? Oh,' " recalled the Rev. Wilmoth. But friends and neighbors said the interracial nature of their marriage is irrelevant. They said Ginni Thomas's family welcomed Clarence Thomas from the beginning. "If you have any feelings about black color, you forget about it as soon as you start talking to him," her father, Donald Lamp, was quoted as saying in the Omaha World-Herald."
item.dc:creator: Sensiblyinteresting
